spec_match.rationale: Wraps AbortController as AsyncDisposable so scope exit cancels every child fetch — exact ergonomic improvement the proposal enables.

interactive.rationale: Mock slow API + in-flight cancellation visible in the log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises 'scope ends, all child fetches abort'; delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Shows disposer-driven abort reasons and an already-aborted scope so visitors can attribute cancellation through AbortSignal.reason.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables are used for the timeline states and the raw inline color styles were removed.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp 1.1.1 on Chrome Canary: started a scope, triggered early async-disposer abort, and observed both simulated requests abort with last reason 'scope ended by async disposer'; reset and ran the already-aborted scope, which showed signal.reason immediately. Console and network were clean.
